text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared main repository. Each time code is added, an automated build process is triggered, compiling the new code to check for errors. This is followed by running tests, such as unit and integration tests, automatically. The main goal is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems. All of these steps are organized within a CI pipeline, which runs each change through a predefined series of checks. This approach helps teams maintain code quality and work together more efficiently.
